taurine.E1_basis: GTEx(computed): addresses the strongest RISING axis (senescence, CDKN2A r=+0.29 FDR≈0) and a declining mito-biogenesis node (ESRRA r=-0.21).
taurine.E2_basis: FACT PMID 37289866 (Science 2023): causal INTERVENTION — taurine declines with age (mouse/monkey/human); restoration ↑healthspan+lifespan, direction=restore.
taurine.E3_basis: FACT PMID 37289866: reduced cellular senescence, suppressed mitochondrial dysfunction, decreased DNA damage, attenuated inflammaging.
taurine.E4_basis: FACT: completed anti-aging RCT NCT05149716 (taurine vs placebo); oral.

glycine_NAC.E1_basis: GTEx(computed): DIRECT fit to the strongest DECLINING axis — glutathione machinery GCLM/GSR/SOD2 all FDR<0.001. GlyNAC = glutathione precursors.
glycine_NAC.E2_basis: INFERENCE: RCT-grade (not genetic). Direction=restore glutathione. NOTE composition overlap with 1C-MIM (glycine; NAC≈cysteine).
glycine_NAC.E3_basis: FACT PMID 33783984/35975308: ↑glutathione, ↓oxidative stress, ↓mito dysfunction, ↓inflammation, ↑muscle strength in older adults (RCT).
glycine_NAC.E4_basis: FACT: completed oral trials in older adults — NCT02348762 (Energetics and Function in Older Humans), NCT01870193 (Glutathione and Fuel Oxidation in Aging); Phase 4 NCT06083480. Safe, low-cost.

nicotinamide_riboside.E1_basis: GTEx(computed): NAD biosynthesis NMNAT1 DECLINES (r=-0.10 FDR=0.019) — one node, weaker than redox axis.
nicotinamide_riboside.E2_basis: INFERENCE: NAD decline in aging is established (NR restores muscle NAD metabolome, PMID 31412242); no candidate-specific muscle-aging MR exists for NR (metabolome-wide sarcopenia MR PMID 38366876 does NOT report an NAD-precursor hit). Direction=restore NAD.
nicotinamide_riboside.E3_basis: FACT PMID 31412242 (Cell Rep 2019): NR ↑NAD metabolome + anti-inflammatory transcriptomic signature in AGED HUMAN skeletal muscle. NOTE: same study reports 'without altering mitochondrial bioenergetics' — NR is NOT credited a mito-quality-control point.
nicotinamide_riboside.E4_basis: FACT: oral RCTs incl Phase 4 NCT05483465 (1 g/d oral used in 31412242); safe.

urolithin_A.E1_basis: GTEx(computed): mitophagy genes (PINK1/BNIP3) not significant decliners here; mito-biogenesis mixed — indirect fit.
urolithin_A.E2_basis: INFERENCE: RCT-level, not genetic; direction=restore mitophagy.
urolithin_A.E3_basis: FACT PMID 35584623 (Cell Rep Med 2022, NCT03464500, MIDDLE-AGED adults): ↑strength ~12%, ↑endurance/6MWT, ↓acylcarnitines, ↓CRP. CAVEAT: pre-registered PRIMARY endpoint (peak power) was NOT met; strength was secondary.

spermidine.E1_basis: GTEx(computed) COUNTER-EVIDENCE: polyamine-synthesis genes DHPS/SMS/SRM INCREASE with age (DHPS r=+0.20 FDR≈0) — endogenous polyamine flux is not declining; against a 'restore' rationale.
spermidine.E2_basis: INFERENCE PMID 27841876 (Nat Med 2016): lifespan/cardioprotection is CARDIAC/organismal, not muscle-aging-specific.
spermidine.E3_basis: FACT PMID 27841876: autophagy/mitophagy induction, cardioprotection, lifespan extension.
spermidine.E4_basis: FACT: trials exist (NCT06186102 Phase2 elderly CAD) but muscle-aging precedent thin.
